title: macrolearia × traillii
intro: Macrolearia × traillii, also known as Traill's twinflower, is a rare, deciduous shrub native to North America, resulting from the hybridization of the Lonicera canadensis and Lonicera ciliosa species. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Macrolearia × traillii include its ability to soothe sore throats, relieve congestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as a treatment for skin irritations.
